description: This example demonstrates how to configure multi-dimensional slicing for 4D or higher dimensional data

example_1_basic_4d.comment: 4D data (samples, height, width, channels) with one dimension specified

explanation.show_md_menu: Enable multi-dimensional slicing UI (required for 4D+ data)
explanation.data_slicing_dimension_0: Specifies sample dimension - this will be excluded from combinations
explanation.md_combinations: System will compute combinations of remaining dims (1,2,3) in groups of 2 (ndim-2 since dim 0 is specified)

md_default.combo_index: Default combination to use (0 = first combination)
md_default.dim_X: Default slice position for dimension X (0-indexed)

example_2_4d_no_specified.comment: 4D data with no dimensions specified in data_slicing

explanation.data_slicing_empty: No dimensions specified, so all 4 dimensions available
explanation.md_combinations: System computes combinations in groups of ndim-2 = 2
System computes combinations in groups of ndim-2 = 3 (since 2 dims specified)
explanation.note: With only one combination possible, the dropdown will show but only have one option

example_4_hide_md_menu.comment: 4D data but multi-dimensional menu is hidden (standard behavior)

explanation.show_md_menu_false: Multi-dimensional UI is hidden, standard slicing used
explanation.behavior: Each dimension gets its own navigation control, standard behavior maintained

usage_notes.when_to_use: Use multi-dimensional slicing when data has 4+ dimensions and you want combinatorial slice exploration

show_md_menu.true: Enables multi-dimensional slicing UI with combination selection

md_default_config.combo_index: Which combination to show initially (0-based index)
md_default_config.dim_X: Default slice position for dimension X (0-based)

ui_behavior.combination_dropdown: Select which group of dimensions to slice
ui_behavior.dimension_controls: For each dimension in selected combination, Previous/Next buttons to navigate slices
